title: Surah Al-Tawbah, Surah Al-Nisa and Silver
quote: Learn Sūrah Al-Barāʾah (At-Tawbah), and teach your women Sūrah Al-Nūr. And have them wear silver for adornment.
intro: It is reported that ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb – Allāh be pleased with him – wrote (by way of instructions):
source: Saʿīd b. Manṣūr, Al-Sunan #1003; Al-Bayhaqī, Shuʿab Al-Īmān #2213.
author: ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb

title: Regular Reading of the Quran
quote: I do not like it that a day or night comes upon me without me looking at the speech of Allāh, meaning reading from the muṣḥaf.
intro: It is reported that ʿUthmān b. ʿAffān – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: ʿUthmān b. ʿAffān

title: When the Scholar Talks, and When He is Silent
quote: The true faqīh (scholar) is the one who the fear of Allāh makes talk and the fear of Allāh makes silent. If he speaks it is by the Book and the Sunnah, and if he is silent, it is by the Book and the Sunnah. And if something is confusing to him and unclear, he withholds judgement and refers it back to whoever knows about it.
intro: It is reported that Al-Fuḍayl b. ʿAyyāḍ – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Al-Fuḍayl b. ʿAyyāḍ

title: Being Selective about Going Out
quote: Do not leave your house a lot, except for something that you must do. And do not sit in a gathering in which you do not benefit some knowledge.
intro: It is reported that Imām Mālik said to one of his companions:
author: Imām Mālik

title: Four of the Worst of the Major Sins
quote: The worst of the worst (major) sins are: to associate partners with Allāh, to lose hope of the aid of Allāh, to despair of the mercy of Allāh, and to feel safe from the plans of Allāh.
intro: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Al-Ṭabarī, Al-Tafsīr (Qurān 4: 31). Graded ṣaḥīḥ by Ibn Kathīr in his tafsīr of the same verse.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd

title: Evil Equality
quote: People will remain upon good as long as they vary (some are more knowledgeable, righteous etc); when they become equal, that is when they are destroyed.
intro: It is reported that Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī

title: The Viral Sin
quote: We used to narrate: Whoever faults his brother for a sin he has repented to Allāh from, Allāh will try him with that very sin.
intro: It is reported that Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Aḥmad b. Ḥanbal, Al-Zuhd p342 (Al-ʿIlmīyah ed., 1983).
author: Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī

title: The Famous Fake
quote: A person has not been true to Allāh, the mighty and sublime, if he loves recognition (fame).
intro: It is reported that Ibrāhīm b. Adham – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Ibrāhīm b. Adham

title: Remembering Your Sins
quote: Everyone should have times when they sit alone, remember their sins, and thus seek [Allāh’s] forgiveness for them.
intro: It is reported that Masrūq – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Masrūq

title: Benefiting from Happiness and Sadness
quote: Everyone gets happy and gets sad, but you should turn your happiness into gratitude (to Allāh) and your sadness into patience (ṣabr).
intro: It is reported that ʿIkrimah – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: ʿIkrimah

title: Minding What You Say
quote: There is no one who is mindful of what he says except that you will see the good of it in the rest of his actions.
intro: It is reported that Yūnus b. ʿUbayd – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn Abī Al-Dunyā, Al-Ṣamt wa Ādāb Al-Lisān article 60.
author: Yūnus b. ʿUbayd

title: Changing Colours
quote: True misguidance is that you approve now what you used to repudiate before (because it was wrong) and repudiate now what you used to approve before; and be aware of changing colours in the religion, for the religion of Allāh is one.
intro: It is reported that Ḥudhayfah b. Al-Yamān – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Ibn Baṭṭah, Al-Ibānatu Al-Kubrā ḥadīth 25, and others.
author: Ḥudhayfah b. Al-Yamān

title: The Companions did Qunut in Witr before the Ruku
quote: Ibn Masʿūd and [the rest of] the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ used to say the qunūt supplication before bowing in the witr prayer.
intro: ʿAlqamah reports:
source: Ibn Abī Shaybah, Al-Muṣannaf ḥadīth 6911, and others.
author: The Ṣaḥabah

title: Four Rewarding Exhortations about People
quote: A man once asked Ubay b. Kaʿb – Allāh be pleased with him, “Exhort me with something I can benefit by and by which I will be rewarded.” He replied, “Be brotherly with brothers according to how righteous they are, do not expend your speech on those who are not interested in it, do not seek anyone to meet your need who does not care if he does not fulfil it, and do not envy the living except for something you would envy the dead.”
intro: Al-Muzanī reports: I heard Al-Shāfiʿī say:
source: Al-Ājurrī, Juz fīhi Ḥikāyāt ʿan Al-Shāfiʿī article 24.
author: Imām Al-Shāfiʿī

title: Sinning in the Time of Calamities
quote: Total destruction lies in doing bad deeds during a time of calamity and tribulation.
intro: It is reported that Al-Nuʿmān b. Bashīr – Allāh be pleased with him – said during a sermon he was delivering in Homs:
author: Al-Nuʿmān b. Bashīr

title: Worship in the Time of Plague
quote: It once reached us in Kūfā that Masrūq used to flee from the plague. Muḥammad denied this and said, “Let us go to his wife and ask her.” So we entered upon her and asked her about this, and she replied, “No, by Allāh, he never used to flee, but he would say, ‘These are days of preoccupation, so I like to be alone during them to worship.’ So he used to go aside and be alone to do worship. Sometimes, I would sit behind him and cry because of what I saw him doing to himself; he would pray so much that his feet would swell. I heard him say, ‘the plague, abdominal disease, postpartum bleeding and drowning: whoever dies during any of them will have it in his credit as martyrdom.'”
intro: It is reported from Anas b. Sīrīn that he said:
author: Masrūq

title: Four Corruptions
quote: “O people, there are four characteristics to come, whoever can avoid reaching them should do so.” [People] asked, “And what are they?” He replied, “There will come a time in which falsehood will rise, and a time will come in which a man will say, ‘By Allāh, I do not know what I am’, he will neither live nor die upon insight (knowledge of what he should be). And a time will come in which a man will start his day on one religion and end it on another. And a time will come when a man will be given wealth from the wealth Allāh provides, in return for saying what is wrong and false and for which Allāh will be angry with him.”
intro: It is reported that Muʿādh b. Jabal – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: Muʿādh b. Jabal

title: Fast Times
quote: How fast these days are in doing away with our lifespans, and how fast this year is in doing away with its months, and how fast this month is in doing away with its days.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Al-Mubārak – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn Abī Al-Dunyā, Al-ʿUmr was Al-Shayb article 25.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Al-Mubārak

title: Do Not Hook People to Bid’ah
quote: Whoever hears of a bidʿah, let him not tell it to those who are with him, lest he cast it into their hearts.
intro: It is reported that Sufyān Al-Thawrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Sufyān Al-Thawrī

title: Do Not Beautify Yourself with Knowledge
quote: Learn knowledge, understand it and benefit from it (live by it); and do not study it in order to decorate yourself with it, for if you live long you will likely see a time when knowledge will be used for beautification like a man beautifies himself with his garments.
intro: It is reported that Ḥabīb b. ʿUbayd – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn Al-Mubārak, Al-Zuhd wa Al-Raqāʾiq no. 1056.
author: Ḥabīb b. ʿUbayd

title: Going to the Seniors, the Wise and the Scholars
quote: Sit with the seniors, mix with the wise, and enquire with the scholars.
intro: It is reported that Abū Juḥayfah – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Ibn Ḥibbān, Rawḍatu Al-ʿUqalāʾ p176. See Al-Albānī, Al-Ḍaʿīfah ḥadīth 3462.
author: Abū Juḥayfah

title: Learning What Not to Learn
quote: Learn what is not to be taken, like you learn what is to be taken.
intro: It is reported that Imām Al-Awzāʿī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn ʿAbd Al-Barr, Jāmiʿu Bayyān Al-ʿIlm article 435.
author: Al-Awzāʿī

title: The Argumentative Loser
quote: If you see a man being obstinate, argumentative and impressed with his own opinion, then his loss is complete.
intro: It is reported that Bilāl b. Saʿd – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Bilāl b. Saʿd

title: Being Humble When Blessed
quote: Whoever has been given wealth, beauty, garments or knowledge and then is not humble will have severe repercussions on the Day of Resurrection.
intro: It is reported that Qatādah – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn Abī Al-Dunyā, Al-Tawāḍuʿ Wa Al-Khumūl ḥadīth 90.
author: Qatādah

title: Praying for the Newborn
quote: When Iyās was born I invited a group of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ and fed them, and they supplicated. I said, “You have all supplicated so may Allāh bless you in what you have supplicated for, and when I make a supplication please say āmīn.” So I supplicated much for him (my newborn) for his religion, his intelligence and the like, and verily now I recognize in him [the qualities] in the supplication of that day.
intro: Muʿāwiyah b. Qurrah – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Al-Bukhārī, Al-Adab Al-Mufrad ḥadīth 1255. Al-Albānī grades its chain of transmission ṣaḥīḥ in Ṣaḥīḥ Al-Adab Al-Mufrad 950/1255.
author: Muʿāwiyah b. Qurrah

title: The Link Between Effort and Success
quote: Knowledge only comes from learning, and forbearance comes from trying to be forbearing, and whoever seeks what is good will be given it, and whoever avoids evil will be protected from it.
intro: The Companion Abū Al-Dardāʾ – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Abū Khaythumah, Kitāb Al-ʿIlm (Albānī edition) ḥadīth 114. Al-Albānī grades its chain of transmission ṣaḥīḥ, and states it has been reported from the Prophet ﷺ. See Al-Ṣaḥīḥah 342.
author: Abū Al-Dardāʾ

title: Consuming without Extravagance or Haughtiness
quote: Wear what you wish and eat what you wish, as long as you avoid two practices: extravagance and haughtiness.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAbbās – Allāh be pleased with them – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAbbās

title: Wearing the Jilbab is an Obligation on the Free, Believing Women
quote: A female slave once entered upon ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb – Allāh be pleased with him – and he recognised her as having been owned by some of the Muhājirūn or the Anṣār, but she was wearing a jilbāb covering her head with it, so he asked her, “Have you been freed?” To which she replied, “No.” So he said, “Then why the jilbāb? Remove it from your head, the jilbāb is only to be worn as a duty by the free women of the believers.” She hesitated and was slow to comply so he raised his stick and hit her on the head with it until she cast [the jilbāb] from her head.
intro: Anas b. Mālik – Allāh be pleased with him – reports:
source: Ibn Abī Shaybah, Al-Muṣannaf ḥadīth 6240. Graded ṣaḥīḥ according to the conditions of Muslim by Al-Albānī in Irwāʾ Al-Ġalīl 6:204.
author: ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb

title: The Companion that Stays
quote: The example of the son of Adam and death is that of a man who has three friends. He says to one of them: “What do you have?” [The first friend] replies, “I have your wealth, take from it what you wish, and whatever you do not take, you will no longer possess.” Then he asks the second, “What do you have?” He replies, “I will look after you, and when you die, I will bury you and then leave you.” The man then asks the third, “What do you have?” He replies, “I will be with you wherever you go.” [Al-Nuʿmān] said, “As for the first one, it is the wealth of a person: whatever he uses is his, and whatever he does not use is not. As for the second, it is a man’s family and kin: when he dies they take care of him, then they leave him. As for the third, it is a man’s actions: wherever he goes (before and after death), they go with him.”
intro: It is reported that Al-Nuʿmān b. Bashīr – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: Al-Nuʿmān b. Bashīr

title: Asking Allah for Good Dreams
quote: O Allāh, verily I ask you for good dreams: dreams that are true and not false, beneficial and not harmful. اللهم إني أسألك رؤيا صالحةً صادقةً غيرَ كاذبةٍ نافعةً غيرَ ضارةٍ And when she said this, [those who heard her] knew that she would not speak any more until she woke up from the night.
intro: Ibn Al-Sunnī records that when ʿĀʾishah – Allāh be pleased with her – wanted to go to sleep she would say:
source: Al-Nawawī, Al-Adhkār ḥadīth 271. Ibn Ḥajr graded it ṣaḥīḥ in Natāʾij Al-Afkār 3:89.
author: ʿĀʾishah

title: If You Want Your Prayer to Benefit You
quote: If you want your ṣalāh to benefit you, say (to yourself): perhaps I will never pray another (my life will end before the next prayer).
intro: It is reported that Bakr b. ʿAbdillāh Al-Muzanī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn Abī Al-Dunyā, Qaṣr Al-ʾAmal article 104.
author: Bakr b. ʿAbdillāh Al-Muzanī

title: Praying for the Tawbah of the Sinner
quote: If you see a man amongst you having committed a sin, do not supplicate Allāh against him and do not revile him; instead, pray to Allāh to cure him and accept his repentance, for when we used to see a man die upon something good, we would have hope for him, and when we used to see a man die upon wrongdoing, we would fear for him.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Ibn Abī Al-Dunyā, Kitāb Al-Tawbah article 112.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd

title: Praise and Blame without Knowledge
quote: No man speaks well about another while not knowing it to be true except that he is also likely to speak badly about him without knowing it to be true. And two people do not accompany each other doing something that is not obedience to Allāh except that it is likely they will part from each other doing something that is not obedience to Allāh.
intro: It is reported that ʿAlī b. Al-Ḥusayn b. ʿAlī b.ʾAbī Ṭālib – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir Al-ʿIlm 5:274 article 2118.
author: ʿAlī b. Ḥusayn Zayn Al-ʿĀbidīn

title: The Value of Most People’s Judgements
quote: Since I have come to know people, I do not rejoice when they praise and I do not dislike it when they blame, for those that praise go to excess in praise and those that blame go to excess in blaming. When the scholar learns knowledge in order to act, it humbles him; but if he learns it for something other than action, it only makes him more boastful.
intro: It is reported that Mālik b. Dīnār – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Mālik b. Dīnār

title: How to Become the Most Devout, Careful and Richest Person
quote: Fulfil what Allāh has obligated you to do and you will be from the most devout of people, stay away from what Allāh has forbidden you and you will be from the most careful of people, and be satisfied with what Allāh has apportioned for you and you will be from the richest of people.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd

title: Faulty Iman
quote: Son of Ādam, you will never find the reality of īmān as long as you fault people for doing what you yourself are guilty of, until you clear yourself of that fault and correct it. Then, you will not correct a fault in yourself except that you will notice another, so your occupation will become correcting yourself, and this is the most beloved thing to Allāh, when you are like this.
intro: It is reported that Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī said:
source: Al-Khaṭīb Al-Baġdādī, Al-Muntakhab min Al-Zuhd wa Al-Raqāʾiq, article 41.
author: Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī

title: The Importance of Tolerating the Anger of the Scholar
quote: There were two men who used to visit Al-Aʿmash, one who was concerned with ḥadīth and one who was not. One day Al-Aʿmash became angry with the man who studied ḥadīth, so the other said to him, “If he ever got angry with me like he got angry with you I would never go back to him.” To this Al-Aʿmash said, “In that case he would be a fool like you, leaving what benefits him because of my bad character (behaviour).”
intro: It is reported from Imām Al-Shāfiʿī:
author: Al-Aʿmash

title: Truthfulness Expels Sin & Lying Expels Goodness
quote: Verily a man continues to be truthful until he is written with Allāh as a true believer (ṣiddīq); and he persists in truthfulness until there is not even the space of a needle for sinfulness left in his heart to settle. And a man lies and persists in lying until there is not even the space of a needle for goodness in his heart to settle.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd

title: The Status of Dutifulness to the Mother [Tawbah]
quote: A man once came to Ibn ʿAbbās and said, “I proposed to a woman and she refused to marry me, then someone else proposed to her and she wanted to marry him; so I became jealous and killed her. Can I repent from this?” [Ibn ʿAbbās] asked, “Is your mother alive?” He replied, “No.” So he said, “Repent (tawbah) to Allāh the mighty and majestic, and worship him as much as you can.” ʿAṭāʾ said, “So I went and asked Ibn ʿAbbās, ‘Why did you ask him if his mother was alive?’ He replied, ‘I do not know of any action that brings one closer to Allāh than dutifulness to the mother.'”
intro: ʿAṭāʾ b. Yasār reports:
source: Al-Bukhārī, Al-Adab Al-Mufrad ḥadīth 4. Graded ṣaḥīḥ by Al-Albānī. See Ṣaḥīḥ Al-Adab Al-Mufrad, Chapter on Dutifulness to the Mother; Al-Ṣaḥīḥah 6:711 ḥadīth 2799.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAbbās

title: Three Men and Three Women
quote: There are three types of men and three types of women. As for the women, then [one] is the chaste, Muslim, gentle, loving and childbearing woman. She helps her family in difficult situations and does not help situations to overcome her family. Rarely will you find such women. Another is a vessel, she does nothing more than bear children. The third type is a shackle, Allāh puts her around the neck of whomever he wills, and when he wills to remove it, he removes it. Men are [also] three kinds. [The first is] a chaste, easy, gentle man who possesses opinion (insight) and is worthy of being consulted; and when a matter befalls him, he follows his insight, and approaches matters as they should be. [The second is] a man who has no opinions of his own, and when a matter befalls him he goes to the one who does have insight and should be consulted, and he adopts his opinion on the matter. The third is a man who is confused, lost and aimless; he neither follows (seeks) guidance, nor obeys any guide.
intro: It is reported that ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb

title: In the House of Ali and Fatimah
quote: We only used to own a single ram’s skin (leather mat), we used to sleep on one end of it and Fāṭimah used to knead dough on the other end.
intro: It is reported that ʿAlī b. Abī Ṭālib – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: Fāṭimah

title: A Man Should Sit Where He Benefits
quote: ʿAlī b. Ḥusayn – Allāh have mercy on him – would sometimes ride to Makkah and return without entering it (meeting anyone there), and he used to sit with Aslam the freedman of ʿUmar (himself a scholar), so it was said to him, “You leave Quraysh, and instead sit with the slave of Banī ʿAdī!” So he replied, “A man [should] sit where he benefits.”
intro: It is reported that:
author: ʿAlī b. Ḥusayn Zayn Al-ʿĀbidīn

title: The Foolish Gathering
quote: Do not sit with the foolish using your knowledge, and do not sit with the knowledgeable (scholars) using your foolishness.
intro: It is reported that Muʿāwiyah b. Qurrah – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Muʿāwiyah b. Qurrah

title: I Would Rather Sing a Song
quote: “Ibn Masʿūd used to say such-and-such [about this issue].” The man asked, “But tell me your opinion.” He replied, “Are you not all astonished by this man? I inform him of what Ibn Masʿūd [said] and he asks me my opinion. My religion is more important to me than that. By Allāh, I would rather sing a song than inform you of my opinion.”
intro: It is reported that a man once came to Al-Shaʿbī – Allāh have mercy on him – and asked him about something, so he replied:
author: Al-Shaʿbī

title: Hidden Safety
quote: Safety lies in not wanting to be known.
intro: It is reported that Sufyān Al-Thawrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Sufyān Al-Thawrī

title: The Diagnosis and the Cure
quote: Verily the Qurān guides you to your disease and your treatment: as for your disease, it is your sins; and as for your treatment, it is to seek the forgiveness of Allāh.
intro: It is reported that Qatādah – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Qatādah

title: Four Requirements for Seeking Knowledge
quote: The seeking of knowledge cannot truly be achieved except with four things: time (al-farāgh, being free to study), sufficient wealth (al-māl, to avoid preoccupation with seeking a living), preservation (al-ḥifẓ, memorization) and piety (al-waraʿ, religious cautiousness).
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Al-Mubārak – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Al-Mubārak

title: Three Traits of the Scholar
quote: You cannot be a scholar unless you have three traits: you do not transgress against those above you, you do not look down on those lesser than you, and you do not take any dunyā in return for your knowledge.
intro: It is reported that Abū Ḥāzim Salamah b. Dīnār – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Abū Ḥāzim Salamah b. Dīnār

title: One Bad Quality Can Spoil Numerous Good Ones
quote: A man might have ten qualities, nine of them good and one bad, but the nine good ones can be spoiled by the one bad quality. Beware of the slips and faults of youth.
intro: It is reported that ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb – Allāh be pleased with him – said to a young man while exhorting him:
author: ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb

title: Four Things the Wise do Not Trust
quote: The insightful and wise do not trust that they are safe from four things: a past sin about which it is not known what the Lord Almighty will do, the remaining lifespan wherein it is not known what destruction lies, some (apparent) advantage a person is given but which could be a lure (from Allāh) in recompense for his wrongdoing, a misguidance that has been beautified – so that he thinks it is guidance – and a momentary deviation of the heart, for a person can be stripped of his religion without realizing it.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Al-Mubārak – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Al-Mubārak

title: Arguing with a Scholar or an Ignoramus
quote: Beware of dispute and argumentation about the religion, and do not argue with a scholar nor an ignoramus. As for the scholar, he will withhold his knowledge from you, and will not be concerned with what you do. As for the ignorant person, he will only cause roughness in your heart and he will not obey you [anyway].
intro: Yūnus reports: Maymūn b. Mihrān once wrote to me saying:
author: Maymūn b. Mihrān

title: The Rarity of Zuhd in Leadership
quote: I have not seen less zuhd (abstinence) in anything than leadership; you can see a man renouncing food, drink, wealth and clothing, but if his leadership is contested, he vehemently defends and has enmity over it.
intro: It is reported that Sufyān Al-Thawrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Sufyān Al-Thawrī

title: Shaving the Head After Al-Udhiyah (Qurbani) on Eid
quote: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿUmar once sacrificed an uḍḥiyah in Al-Madīnah. He told me to buy a horned ram for him, and then to slaughter it on the Day of Aḍḥā at the people’s prayer place (muṣallā), so I did that. Then it was taken to ʿAbdullāh b. ʿUmar, and he shaved his head once the ram was slaughtered. He was ill at the time and had not witnessed the ʿEid with the people.
intro: Nāfiʿ reports:
source: Al-Imām Mālik, Al-Muwaṭṭā, Book of Sacrificial Slaughters, Chapter on What is Recommended of the Sacrifices.
author: Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī

title: Benefitting from Being Happy with what Allah has Provided
quote: Whoever is happy (satisfied) with what Allāh has apportioned for him, it will suffice him and Allāh will bless it for him, but whoever is not satisfied, then [what is apportioned for him] will not suffice him and it will not be blessed.
intro: It is reported that Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn Abī Al-Dunyā, Kitāb Al-Riḍā ʿan Allāh bi Qaḍāʾihi article 95.
author: Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī

title: Jafar Al-Sadiq on Abu Bakr and Umar
quote: I asked Abū Jaʿfar and his son Jaʿfar (Al-Ṣādiq) about Abū Bakr and ʿUmar, so he replied, “O Sālim, love and ally yourself with them, and dissociate yourself from their enemy, for they were imāms of guidance.” Then he said, “O Sālim, would a man revile his own grandfather? Abū Bakr is a grandfather of mine. May the intercession of Muḥammad ﷺ not reach me on the Day of Resurrection if I do not love and ally myself with them and disassociate from their enemy.”
intro: It is reported from Sālim b. Abī Ḥafṣah:
author: Jaʿfar Al-Ṣādiq

title: The Importance of Knowing Different Opinions Before Giving Fatwa
quote: Whoever has not heard the differences of opinion, then do not consider him a scholar.
intro: Saʿīd b. Abī ʿArūbah – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn ʿAbd Al-Barr, Jāmiʿ Bayān Al-ʿIlm wa Faḍlihī no. 1521.
author: Imām Mālik

title: Umar on Trustworthiness
quote: Do not expose yourself to what does not concern you, stay away from your enemy, beware of [taking as] your friend anyone except the trustworthy amongst people – and there is no one who is trustworthy except one who fears Allāh; and do not accompany the sinner in case you learn his sinfulness, and do not let him know your secrets, and consult those who fear Allāh for your affairs.
intro: It is reported that ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb

title: Two Qualities of the Noble
quote: A man does not become noble until he has two qualities: he is undesirous of what other people possess, and he pardons and overlooks them.
intro: It is reported that Ayyūb Al-Sakhtiyānī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Ayyūb Al-Sakhtiyānī

title: Knowing what Spoils Your Deeds
quote: A man remains upon good as long as he knows what things spoil his deeds.
intro: It is reported that Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī

title: Qualities of Those Who Fuel Fitnah
quote: Do not be from those who are hasty, who broadcast (what should not be broadcast), who plant trouble by divulging what should be secret, for you will face severe, prolonged and heavy tribulations.
intro: ʿAlī b. Abī Ṭālib – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Al-Bukhārī, Al-Adab Al-Mufrad no. 327. Its chain of transmission was graded ṣaḥīḥ by Al-Albānī in Ṣaḥīḥ Al-Adab Al-Mufrad no.250.
author: ʿAlī b. Abī Ṭālib

title: The Danger of Over-Suspicion
quote: The victim of theft might continue to suspect and conjecture [about who stole from him] until he becomes worse [in sin] than the thief.
intro: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Al-Bukhārī, Al-Adab Al-Mufrad no. 1289. Its chain of transmission was graded ṣaḥīḥ by Al-Albānī in Ṣaḥīḥ Al-Adab Al-Mufrad no.974.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd

title: Three Things to Satisfy Yourself With
quote: There are three things that I like for myself and for my brothers: That the Muslim man looks to the Qurān; he learns it, recites it, ponders it and refers to it. Second, that he looks to the narrations and the Sunnah; he asks about it and follows it with all his efforts. Third, that he leaves alone all these people except when doing good.
intro: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAwn – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Al-Bukhāri, Al-Ṣaḥīḥ, no. 97 (in annotative, discontiguous form). The chain of transmission is connected in Al-Marwazī, Al-Sunnah, no. 108.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAwn

title: Praising Oneself with Iman
quote: A man once came to ʿAbdullāh [ibn Masʿūd] – Allāh be pleased with him – and said, “We were once travelling when we met another group of travellers, so we asked them, ‘Who are you?’ They replied, ‘We are the believers!'” So [Ibn Masʿūd] said, “So why didn’t they just say, ‘We are from the people of Jannah?!'”
intro: Abū Wāʾil reports:
source: Abū ʿUbayd Al-Qāsim b. Sallām, Kitāb Al-Īmān, article 11. Shaykh Al-Albānī graded the chain of transmission of this and the following narration ṣaḥīḥ according to the standard of Bukhārī and Muslim.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd

title: The Lucrative Brother
quote: A brother who, whenever you meet him, points out to you one of your faults (for you to correct yourself) is better for you than a brother who, whenever you meet him, puts a dīnār (money) in your hand.
intro: It is reported that Bilāl b. Saʿd – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir Al-ʿIlm no. 1428.
author: Bilāl b. Saʿd

title: Lying and Iman
quote: Beware of lying, for lying is far removed from īmān.
intro: It is reported that Abū Bakr Al-Ṣiddīq – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Ḵallāl, Al-Sunnah no. 1467, 1470; Al-Bayhaqī, Shuʿab Al-Īmān no. 4463.
author: Abū Bakr Al-Ṣiddīq

title: Three People to Stay Away From
quote: Do not be with the sinner (fājir), for he will beautify to you the things he does, and he will want you to be like him; and he will beautify to you the worst of his practices; and his entrance upon you and leaving from your company will cause ignominy and discredit [of you]. And do not accompany the fool (aḥmaq), for he will exhaust himself [to help you] but will not benefit you, and he may want to benefit you but end up harming you; his silence is better than his speaking, his distance is better than his closeness, and him dying is better than him living. And do not accompany the liar, for life will not benefit you with him, he will tell others what you say, and tell you what others say; and if you speak the truth, it will not be believed.
intro: It is reported that ʿAlī b. ʾAbī Ṭālib – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir AlʿIlm no. 1379.
author: ʿAlī b. Abī Ṭālib

title: Being Two Faced to the Muslim Ruler
quote: Some people said to Ibn ʿUmar, “We enter upon our rulers, and we say to them something different to what we say when we leave their company.” He said, “We used to regard this as [a type of] nifāq (hypocrisy).”
intro: Muḥammad b. Zayd b. ʿUmar reports from his father:
source: Al-Bukhārī, Ṣaḥīḥ Al-Bukhārī no. 7178, and others.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿUmar

title: Maymun b. Mihran Describes The Salaf He Met
quote: I have met men in front of whom I would be ashamed to speak, and I have met men who used to say nothing but the truth, or be silent. And I have met men who had never seen the sky properly, out of fear of their Lord.
intro: It is reported that Maymūn b. Mihrān – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir Al-ʿIlm article 2039.
author: Maymūn b. Mihrān

title: The Opportunity of Fasting Ashura
quote: “How comes you fast the Day of ʿĀshūrāʾ when traveling but not so during the fast of Ramaḍān?” He replied, “For Ramaḍān, the number of days can be made up later, but ʿĀshūrāʾ will be missed.”
intro: It is reported that Imām Al-Zuhrī – Allāh have mercy on him – was traveling and fasted ʿĀshūrāʾ, so he was asked:
author: Imām Al-Zuhrī

title: Honouring and Dishonouring Oneself
quote: It used to be said, “People have never honoured themselves with anything [better] than the obedience of Allāh, and they have never dishonoured themselves with anything [worse] than disobedience to Allāh.”
intro: It is reported that Yaḥyā b. Abī Kathīr – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn Abī Al-Dunyā, Muḥāsabatu Al-Nafs article 98.
author: Yaḥyā b. Abī Kathīr

title: Trust in Allāh During Inflation
quote: “O Abū Ḥāzim, do you not see how prices have risen?” He replied, “And what is the worry? Verily, he (Allāh) who provides for us during deflation is the one who will provide for us during inflation.”
intro: It is reported that Abū Ḥāzim Salamah b. Dīnār – Allāh have mercy on him – was once asked:
author: Abū Ḥāzim Salamah b. Dīnār

title: The Blessing of not Being a Kharijite
quote: There are two blessings I have been blessed with, I know not which of them is greatest: that Allāh guided me to Islām, and that he did not make me a Ḥarūrī (from the Khawārij extremists).
intro: It is reported that Abu Al-ʿĀlyah – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Abū Al-ʿĀliyah

title: Two Calamities for Fitnah
quote: Stay away from the sinful amongst the scholars and the ignorant amongst the worshippers, for these two are the calamity of everyone who would fall into fitnah.
intro: It is reported that Al-Shaʿbī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Al-Shaʿbī

title: Evils of Those who Love Authority
quote: There is no one who loves authority (leadership) except that he envies, transgresses, chases the faults of others and dislikes anyone being mentioned in a good light.
intro: It is reported that Al-Fuḍayl b. ʿAyyād – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn ʿAbd Al-Barr, Jāmiʿ Bayān Al-ʿIlm article. 971.
author: Al-Fuḍayl b. ʿAyyāḍ

title: The Correct Place of Opinion
quote: There is no place for anyone’s opinion [when it comes to] what is in the Book (revelation). The opinion of the imāms (great scholars) is only regarding what the Book did not come down with, and regarding which no Sunnah has passed from the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. And the opinion of no one is counted in the face of a Sunnah from the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ.
intro: Al-Awzāʿī reports that Caliph ʿUmar b. ʿAbd Al-ʿAzīz – Allāh have mercy on him – wrote (in his orders):
author: ʿUmar b. ʿAbd Al-ʿAzīz

title: Three Types of Sleep
quote: There are three types of sleep: the incompetent’s sleep, proper sleep, and stupid sleep. As for the incompetent’s sleep, it is to sleep in the morning after sunrise (ḍuḥā time), when people are going about their needs (work), he is sleeping. As for the proper sleep, it is the midday nap (qaylūlah). And as for the stupid sleep, it is to sleep when it is time to pray.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Al-ʿAmr b. Al-ʿĀṣ – Allāh be pleased with them – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAmr

title: A Reported Picture of the Prophet & Abu Bakr from Judaeo-Christian Times
quote: I once travelled to Shām for trade. When I reached lower Shām a man from the People of the Book met me and asked me, “Is there amongst you a man who is a Prophet?” I replied, “Yes.” He then asked, Would you recognise his image if you saw it?” I replied, “Yes.” He then admitted me into a house in which there were images, but I did not see an image of the Prophet ﷺ. While I was there, another man from them entered upon us and asked us “What are you doing?” So we informed him. He then took us to his home, and as soon as I entered I saw the image of the Prophet ﷺ and it showed a man holding on to the heel of the Prophet ﷺ. I asked, “Who is this man holding his heel?” He replied, “There was no prophet except that after him came another prophet, except this prophet, for there is no prophet after him. And this is the khalīfah after him.” And I saw that it looked like Abū Bakr – Allāh be pleased with him.
intro: It is reported from the Companion Jubayr b. Muṭʿim – Allāh be pleased with him – that he said:
source: Ibn Kathīr, Al-Tafsīr, in the commentary of 7:157
author: Jubayr b. Muṭʿim

title: Not Speaking in Front of Elders
quote: I was young during the time of Allāh’s Messenger ﷺ and I had memorised things he had said; nothing stopped me from speaking except for the fact that there were amongst us men who were older than me.
intro: Samurah b. Jundub – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: Abū Bakr b. ʿAyyāsh

title: The Reality of Dhikr and Fear of Allah
quote: Verily, fear (al-khashyah) is that you fear Allāh such that your fear comes between you and your disobedience (of Allāh). That is khashyah. And dhikr (remembrance) is obedience to Allāh: whoever obeys Allāh has remembered Him; and whoever does not obey Him is not a rememberer of Him, even if he says a lot of tasbīḥ and recites a lot of Qurān.
intro: It is reported that Saʿīd b. Jubayr – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Saʿīd b. Jubayr

title: Letting Elders Enter First
quote: Blessings are with your seniors.
intro: Al-Khaṭīb Al-Baghdādī has mentioned this heading and quotes the following general aḥādīth. He then reports narrations from some of the Salaf about letting elders enter first.
source: Ibn Ḥibbān et al. Graded ṣaḥīh by Al-Albānī and others. See Al-Ṣaḥīḥah no. 1778.
author: Isḥāq b. Rāhuwayh

title: Getting Ahead of Yourself
quote: Whoever takes leadership quickly (too early) will harm a lot of [his] knowledge (will not learn what he needs); and whoever does not take leadership can continue to seek knowledge until he reaches [where he needs to be].
intro: It is reported that Sufyān Al-Thawrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Sufyān Al-Thawrī

title: The Best Things a Person Can be Given
quote: “What is the best thing a person is ever given?” He replied, “Innate intelligence.” He then asked, “And if not that?” He replied, “Good conduct.” He then asked, “And if not that?” He replied, “A compassionate brother to consult.” He then asked, “And if not that?” He replied, “Long silence.” He asked, “And if not that?” Ibn Al-Mubārak replied, “Then an early death.”
intro: It is reported from Ḥabīb Al-Jallāb that he asked ʿAbdullāh b. Al-Mubārak – Allāh have mercy on him:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Al-Mubārak

title: Speaking Against the Rulers is the Way of the Persians and Romans
quote: A man from [the tribe of] the Anṣār once came to me during the caliphate of ʿUthmān. He spoke to me and I realised he wanted me to fault ʿUthmān; he spoke at length and he had some heaviness in his speech and could not finish speaking quickly. When he had finished, I said to him, “We used to say, while the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ was still alive, the best of the Ummah of the Messenger of Allāh after him is Abū Bakr, then ʿUmar, then ʿUthmān. And by Allāh, we do not know that ʿUthmān has ever killed anyone without right or committed any major sin; but it is an issue of this wealth [with you people]: if he gives you, you are happy, and if he gives to his kin, you are angry. You only want to be like the Persians and Romans, who leave no leader of theirs without killing him.” Then his eyes filled with tears and he said, “O Allāh, we do not want this.”
intro: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿUmar – Allāh be pleased with them – said:
source: Imām Aḥmad, Faḍāʾil Al-Ṣaḥābah, article 64, except for the last sentence. Shaykh Waṣīyullāh ʿAbbās graded its chain of transmission ṣaḥīḥ in his study of the work.

title: The Correct Reaction to Knowledge
quote: O Ayyūb, when Allāh brings about knowledge for you, bring about worship of him, and do not let your [sole] concern be to narrate it.
intro: Ayyūb Al-Sakhtiyānī reports that Abū Qilābah – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn ʿAbd Al-Barr, Jāmiʿ Bayān Al-ʿIlm, article 1279.
author: Abū Qilābah

title: From the Fruits of Sincerely Seeking Knowledge
quote: Whoever seeks knowledge sincerely, for the servants of Allāh to benefit by and to benefit himself, then being hidden (from fame) is more beloved to him than seeking loftiness. He is the one who becomes more lowly to himself, strives more in worship, fears Allāh more, yearns for Allāh more, and becomes more humble amongst people. He cares not what he has of this dunyā night or day.
intro: It is reported that Ibrāhīm b. Adham – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Ibrāhīm b. Adham

title: Taking Knowledge in Stages
quote: O Yūnus! Do not try to overcome knowledge, for verily knowledge is [like vast] valleys: whichever of them you take will stop you before you traverse it. Instead, take it over the days and nights. And do not take knowledge all at once, for whoever tries to take it all at once will lose it all at once. Rather take it bit by bit over the days and nights.
intro: It is reported that Al-Zuhrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said to Yūnus b. Yazīd:
source: Ibn ʿAbd Al-Barr, Jāmiʿ Bayān Al-ʿIlm article 652.
author: Imām Al-Zuhrī

title: The Danger of Beautifying Mosques and Copies of the Quran
quote: When you adorn your copies of the Qurān and embellish your mosques, destruction will be upon you.
intro: It is reported that Abū Al-Dardāʾ – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Ibn Al-Mubārak, Al-Zuhd wa Al-Raqāʾiq, article 746. Shaykh Al-Albānī stated that the chain of transmission of this narration consists of reliable reporters, except he did not know if the reporter Bakr b. Suwādah had heard directly from Abū Al-Dardāʾ. The narration corroborates a similar ḥadīth reported from the Prophet ﷺ himself. See Al-Ṣaḥīḥah ḥadīth 1351.
author: Abū Al-Dardāʾ

title: Boasting and Pretending to Have Sinned
quote: Boasting that you did a sin (when you did not) is worse than committing the sin.
intro: It is reported that Al-ʿAwwām b. Ḥawshab – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir Al-ʿIlm, article 3073.
author: Al-ʿAwām b. Ḥawshab

title: Dua of the Horse
quote: “What are you doing with this horse of yours?” Abū Ḏarr replied, “I think this horse has had his supplication answered.” Muʿāwiyah asked, “And what supplication does a beast have?” He replied, “By him in whose hand is my soul, there is no horse except that it supplicates every twilight before the dawn: O Allāh, you have given me to a servant of yours and have put my provision at his hands, so make me more beloved to him than his wife, his property and his children.”
intro: Muʿāwiyah b. Khadīj (or Ḥudayj) reports that he once passed by Abū Ḏarr Al-Ġifārī – Allāh be pleased with him – while the latter was standing with a horse of his, so he asked:
author: Abū Dharr Al-Ghifārī

title: How to Look at Yourself and Others
quote: When you see someone older than you, say: he has preceded me in īmān and righteous actions so he is better than me; and when you see someone younger than you, say: I have preceded him in sinning and disobedience so he is better than me. And when you see your brothers honouring and revering you, say: this is a virtue they have attained; and when you see them falling short (in their treatment of you) say: this is [because of] a sin I committed.
intro: It is reported that Bakr b. ʿAbdillāh Al-Muzanī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn Al-Jawzī, Ṣifatu Al-Ṣafwah, article 505: Bakr b. ʿAbdillāh Al-Muzanī.
author: Bakr b. ʿAbdillāh Al-Muzanī

title: Peo­ple Change & Allah For­gives
quote: Do not be hasty in praising people or blaming them, for perhaps what pleases you from a person today will displease you tomorrow, and perhaps what displeases you today, will please you tomorrow. Indeed, people change. It is Allāh who forgives the sins. And Allāh is more merciful to his servant the day he meets him than a mother who lays out a bed for her child in an empty patch of land and feels [the ground]: if there is a risk of being stung, it will be her instead of him (her child), and if there is a risk of being pricked by a thorn, it will be her instead of him.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Al-Bayhaqī, Shuʿab Al-Īmān article 6177, and others.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd

title: When the Sinful and the Rabble Take Knowledge
quote: The people will not cease to be well as long as they take knowledge from their seniors. When they take it from their juniors and their bad people, they will be destroyed.
intro: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Ibn ʿAbd Al-Barr, Jāmiʿ Bayān Al-ʿIlm article 1057.
author: Sufyān Al-Thawrī

title: The Strangeness of Iman
quote: Nifāq (hypocrisy) used to be something unusual amongst all the Īmān. Soon, Īmān will be something strange amongst all the nifāq.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Al-ʿAmr b. Al-ʿĀṣ – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAmr

title: Not Even if You Have a Response
quote: Do not sit with the people of desires (Bidʿah, heresy), even if you think you have a response [to what they say].
intro: It is reported that Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī

title: We Take Our Religion from the Companions
quote: Sharīk b. ʿAbdillāh came to us around fifty years ago, and we said to him, “O Abū ʿAbdillāh, here amongst us there are people from the Muʿtazilah who reject these āḥādīth [like]: ‘Allāh descends to the lowest heaven’, and ‘the people of Jannah will see their Lord’. So Sharīk narrated to me around ten such narrations, then said: ‘As for us, we have taken our religion from the sons of the Tābiʿīn, from the Ṣaḥābah. Who have they taken from?’”
intro: ʿAbbād b. Al-ʿAwwām narrates:
source: Al-Ḏahabī, Al-ʿUluw. Graded ṣaḥīḥ by Al-Albānī in Mukhtaṣar Al-ʿUluw article 146.
author: Ṣaḥābah

title: The Whole Year for Ramadan
quote: They (the Salaf) used to supplicate to Allāh for six months asking Him to get them to the month of Ramaḍān; and they used to supplicate for six months that Allāh accept [their fasting and other worship in Ramaḍān].
intro: It is reported that Muʿlā b. Al-Faḍl – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Abul-Qāsim Al-Aṣbahānī, Al-Targhīb wa Al-Tarhīb article 1761.
author: The Salaf

title: Why is Knowledge Learned?
quote: Knowledge is only learned to [apply it and] put into practice the Taqwā of Allāh the mighty and sublime.
intro: It is reported that Sufyān Al-Thawrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir Al-ʿIlm article 943.
author: Sufyān Al-Thawrī

title: If a Person is Exposed, It’s not his First Sin
quote: A young man who had stolen was brought to ʿUmar (for punishment). He said, “By Allāh I have never stolen before this time.” So ʿUmar responded, “You lie, Allāh would not (or does not) surrender a servant of His on the first sin.”
intro: Anas b. Mālik reports:
source: Abū Dāwūd, Al-Zuhd article 56, and others. Graded ṣaḥīh by Ibn Kathīr and others.
author: ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb

title: The Worst Fitnah
quote: “What is the worst fitnah?” He replied, “That good and evil is presented to you and you do not know which of them to follow.”
intro: It is reported that Ḥudhayfah – Allāh be pleased with him – was asked:
source: Ibn Abī Shaybah, Al-Muṣannaf, Kitāb Al-Fitan, article 38565.
author: Ḥudhayfah b. Al-Yamān

title: The Reason Behind Forgetting Quran
quote: No one who has learned Qurān and then forgotten it except due to a sin he has committed, because Allāh the most high said: وَمَآ أَصَـٰبَكُم مِّن مُّصِيبَةٍ فَبِمَا كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ
intro: It is reported that Al-Ḍaḥḥāk b. Muzāḥim, the famous scholar of tafsīr from the Tābiʿīn, said:
author: Al-Ḍaḥḥāk b. Muzāḥim

title: Roasted Skins and Stone
quote: A man amongst the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ would go three days without finding anything to eat, so he would take some animal skin, roast it and eat that. If he found nothing at all, he would tie a rock to himself to straighten his back.
intro: Muḥammad b. Sīrīn – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Al-Mundhirī, Al-Tarġīb wa Al-Tarhīb. Graded ḥasan by Al-Albānī. See Ṣaḥīḥ Al-Tarġīb wa Al-Tarhīb article 3310.
author: Muḥammad b. Sīrīn

title: The Zuhd of Abu Ubaydah and the Tears of Umar
quote: When ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb arrived in Al-Shām, he said to Abū ʿUbaydah – Allāh be pleased with them: “Take us to your home.” Abū ʿUbaydah said, “And what will you do with my home?” ʿUmar replied, “Just take us there.” Abū ʿUbaydah said, “You only want to cry your eyes out over me.” So he entered his house and saw nothing [by way of furnishings] in it. ʿUmar asked, “Where are your things? I see nothing but rags, a water-skin and a dish (tray), and you are a governor! Do you have food?” So Abū ʿUbaydah went over to an old pail (bucket) and took out some scraps, and ʿUmar began to weep. Abu ʿUbaydah said to him, “I told you you would cry your eyes out over me. O Commander of the Believers, sufficient for you from the dunyā is what delivers you to your place of rest.” ʿUmar said, “The dunyā changed us all except you Abū ʿUbaydah.”
intro: It is reported from ʿAbdullāh b. ʿUmar:
source: Abū Dāwūd, Kitāb Al-Zuhd article 123, and others.
author: Abū ʿUbaydah b. Al-Jarrāh

title: The Beauty in the Quran
quote: Do you tempt the thief, when its beauty is in its inside?
intro: Whenever ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAbbās – Allāh be pleased with them – saw a muṣḥaf (copy of the Qurān) decorated with silver or gold he would say:
source: Abū ʿUbayd Al-Qāsim b. Sallām, Faḍāʾil Al-Qurān article 907.
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAbbās

title: The Link Between Blessings and Gratitude
quote: Blessings arrive with gratitude [to Allāh], and gratitude is connected with more [blessings], and the two are tied together: more blessings from Allāh will never stop unless gratitude from the servant stops.
intro: It is reported that ʿAlī b. Abī Ṭālib – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: ʿAlī b. Abī Ṭālib

title: Three Exhorters
quote: Whoever does not take exhortation [and is not effected] by three things, will not be exhorted by anything: Islām, the Qurān and old age (graying).
intro: It is reported that ʿAbd Al-ʿAzīz b. Abī Rawwād – Allāh have mercy on him – said to a man:
source: Ibn Abī Al-Dunyā, Al-ʿUmr Wa Al-Shayb #40
author: ʿAbd Al-ʿAzīz b. Abī Rawwād

title: Teaching Children To Love Abu Bakr & Umar
quote: The Salaf used to teach their children to love Abū Bakr and ʿUmar like they used to teach them a sūrah of the Qurān.
intro: It is reported that Imām Mālik said:
source: Al-Lālakāʾī, Sharḥ ʾUsūl ʾIʿtiqād Ahl Al-Sunnah #2325.
author: Imām Mālik

title: How Much Shaytan Tries to Cause Marital Strife
quote: Verily Shayṭān comes to your bed after your wife has made it and prepared it and throws a stick, a stone, or something [similar] on it to make the husband angry with his wife. So if someone finds this, let him not be angry with his wife, for it is the work of Shayṭān.
intro: Abū ʾUmāmah – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Al-Bukhārī, Al-Adab Al-Mufrad #1191. The chain of transmission is graded ḥasan by Shaykh Al-Albānī; he also regards it as being authentic from the Prophet ﷺ . See Ṣaḥīḥ Al-Adab Al-Mufrad 1:463
author: Abū ʾUmāmah Al-Bāhilī

title: Three Things that Must be Given to the Righteous and the Sinful
quote: There are three things that must be given to both the righteous and the sinful: the ties of the womb (relatives) must be kept connected, whether they are righteous or sinful; trust must be fulfilled, for the righteous and the sinful; and promises must be kept, to the righteous as well as the sinful.
intro: It is reported that Maymūn b. Mihrān – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Maymūn b. Mihrān

title: Having the Gatherings of Knowledge in the Mosque
quote: The masājid are the sittings of the Prophets, and a protection from Shayṭān.
intro: It is reported that ʿAlī [b. Abī Ṭālib] – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: Abū Idrīs Al-Khawlānī

title: Nine Things to Say Often
quote: We were once in the house of ʿAlqamah b. Qays. Rabīʿ b. Ḵuthaym entered upon us and sat in a corner of the house. Then he said, “Speak only a little except for nine things: subḥānallāh, al-ḥamdulillāh, lā ilāha illallāh, Allāhu ʾakbar, reciting the Qurān, enjoining good, forbidding wrong, asking Allāh for what is good and seeking His protection from harm (evil).”
intro: It is reported that Muḥammad [Ibn Sīrīn] – Allāh have mercy him – said:
author: Rabīʿ b. Khuthaym

title: Broadcasted Sins
quote: When a sin is hidden it harms only the perpetrator, but if it is made apparent and not rectified, it harms all the people.
intro: Bilāl b. Saʿd – Allāh have mercy him – said:
author: Bilāl b. Saʿd

title: Final Blame and Praise
quote: Whoever seeks praise by [saying or doing] what goes against Allāh, Allāh will turn those praises back on him to blame; and whoever is bold enough to [say or do] what brings blame [from people but] is in conformity to the truth, Allāh will turn that blame into praise.
intro: It is reported that Khālid b. Maʿdān – Allāh have mercy him – said:
author: Khālid b. Maʿdān

title: Lofty Manners & Conduct of the Salaf [Examples]
quote: Have taqwā of Allāh and behave modestly (with a sense of shame), and cover yourselves; let not any of you bathe except behind a covering.
intro: It is reported that Jābir b. ʿAbdillāh – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: Al-Aḥnaf b. Qays

title: Lands of Scholars and Doctors
quote: Do not live in a land in which there is neither a scholar to inform you about your religion, nor a doctor to tell you about your body.
intro: It is reported that Imām Al-Shāfiʿī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Imām Al-Shāfiʿī

title: Hafsah on Two Reasons to Marry
quote: My brother, marry, for if you have a child and it dies, it will precede you into Al-Jannah (and be a cause for you to be admitted), and if it lives, the child will supplicate for good for you.
intro: ʿAmr b. Dīnār reports that ʿAbdullāh b. ʿUmar – Allāh be pleased with them – intended not to marry after the [passing of the] Prophet – ṣallallāhu ʿalayhi wa salam, so Ḥafṣah (his sister) – Allāh be pleased with her – advised him:
author: Ḥafṣah

title: Who is Rich?
quote: “Are we not from the poor of the Muhājirīn?” He replied, “Do you have a wife to go back to?” The man replied, “Yes.” ʿAbdullāh asked, “Do you have a home to live in?” The man said, “Yes.” ʿAbdullāh said, “Then you are one of the rich.” The man said, “And I have a servant.” ʿAbdullāh said, “In that case, you are a king!”
intro: A man once asked ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAmr b. Al-ʿĀṣ – Allāh be pleased with them:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAmr

title: Allied Souls
quote: If the people were gathered on one plain, all of them believers, except for two unbelievers amongst them, they would join each other. And if the people were gathered on one plain, all of them unbelievers, except for two believers amongst them, they would join each other.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: Al-Fuḍayl b. ʿAyyāḍ

title: The Behavior of The Seeker of Knowledge
quote: When you learn knowledge, preserve it, and do not mix it with laughter and falsehood, such that the hearts refuse (dislike) it.
intro: It is reported that ʿAlī – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Al-Khaṭīb Al-Baghdādī, Al-Jāmiʿ li-Akhlāq Al-Rāwī wa Ādāb Al-Sāmiʿ 1:232
author: Imām Mālik

title: True Certainty
quote: It is part of sure belief (yaqīn) [in Allāh] that you do not please the people by angering Allāh, and do not praise anyone [else] for what Allāh has provided you, and do not blame anyone for what Allāh has not given you. For verily, the provision of Allāh is not gained by the keenness of those who are keen [for you to have it], nor is it prevented by the hatred of those who hate [for you to have it]. And verily Allāh, in His justice, made happiness and joy in being certain and content [with Allāh], and made worry and sadness in doubt and anger.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd

title: Verbal Security
quote: Leave alone what you have nothing to do with, and do not speak about what does not concern you, and secure your tongue like you secure your money.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAmr b. Al-ʿĀs – Allāh be pleased with them – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAmr

title: The Value of Avoiding Haram Income
quote: It is better for a man to leave one ḥarām dirham than to give one hundred thousand in charity.
intro: It is reported that Mālik b. Dīnār – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir Al-ʿIlm 5:125.
author: Mālik b. Dīnār

title: Knowledge is the Good of this Life
quote: There is no good in this life except for one of two men: a man listening carefully, receiving and holding [knowledge]; or someone knowledgeable speaking.
intro: It is reported that Abū Al-Dardāʾ – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: Abū Al-Dardāʾ

title: Wolves in Sheeps’ Clothing
quote: There will come upon the people a time when their hearts will be derelict of the Qurān; they will find neither sweetness nor pleasure by it. If they fall short of doing what they have been commanded to do, they will say: Allāh is most forgiving, merciful (He will forgive us), and if they do what they have been forbidden from doing, they will say: we will be forgiven, we haven’t committed any shirk with Allāh. Their affairs will all be based on [false] hope, having no truth and sincerity with it. They will wear the skins of lambs over hearts of wolves. The best of them in his religion will be someone who compromises.
intro: It is reported that Abūl-ʿĀliyah said:
author: Abū Al-ʿĀliyah

title: The Scholar, the Ignorant, the Heedless and the Fool
quote: Men are of four types: There is the man who knows and he knows that he knows. He is knowledgeable, so follow him and ask him. And there is the man who does not know, and he knows that he does not know. He is ignorant, so teach him. And there is the man who knows, but he does not know that he knows. He is heedless, so remind him. And then there is the man who does not know, but he does not know that he does not know (he thinks he knows). This is the fool (idiot), so beware of him.
intro: It is reported that Al-Khalīl b. Aḥmad said:
source: Ibn ˁAbd Al-Barr, Jāmiˁ Bayān Al-ˁilm wa Faḍlihī article 1538.
author: Al-Khalīl b. Aḥmad

title: Who Can Feel Secure From Trial?
quote: I entered upon Abū Al-Dardāʾ – Allāh be pleased with him – at his home in Ḥims and found him standing in prayer at his place of prayer. When he sat for tashahhud he started to seek protection with Allāh from al-nifāq (hypocrisy). So when he had finished praying, I said “May Allāh forgive you o Abū Al-Dardāʾ, what have you got to do with nifāq?” He said, “O Allāh forgive!”, three times, and replied, “Who can feel secure from trial, who can feel secure from trial? By Allāh, a man can be tested with fitnah in a single hour and turn away from his religion.”
intro: It is reported that Jubayr b. Nufayr said:
author: Abū Al-Dardāʾ

title: Beware the Sinful Scholar
quote: “Beware of the sinful scholar (al-ʿālim al-fāsiq).” This reached ʿUmar, so he wrote to him in fear, “Who is the sinful scholar?” He wrote back, “I did not intend but good; this is when there is an imām (leader, taken as an example) who speaks knowledge, but practices sins, confusing the people, and thus they go astray.”
intro: It is reported that Harim b. Ḥayyān – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Ḥarim b. Ḥayyān

title: Imam Ahmad on Boycotting the Khawarij
quote: Do not sell food or clothes to them, and do not buy from them. And he said, The Khawārij are renegades, an evil people.
intro: Al-Khallāl records that Imām Aḥmad – may Allāh have mercy on him – said about the Khawārij:
author: Imām Aḥmad

title: Sermon of Uthman on Guidance before Death
quote: O people! Fear Allāh and obey Him, for piety is a valuable prize. Verily the most intelligent person is he who takes account of himself and works for what comes after death, and acquires through the light (guidance) of Allāh a light for the darkness of the grave. The servant of Allāh should fear lest Allāh the Mighty and Sublime resurrects him blind though he used to see. A few comprehensive words can be enough for a wise man, whereas the deaf (who does not listen to guidance) is being called from afar. And know that whoever Allāh the Mighty and Sublime is with has nothing to fear; but whoever Allāh the Mighty and Sublime is against, then in whom can he hope after Allāh?!
intro: It is reported that ʿUthmān b. ʿAffān – Allāh be pleased with him – once addressed the people in a sermon. He praised Allāh, then said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir Al-ʿIlm 4:116,117.
author: ʿUthmān b. ʿAffān

title: Just Playing
quote: Whatever you fool around with, don’t fool around with your dīn (religion).
intro: It is reported that Imām Mālik – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Al-Lālakāʾī, Sharḥ ʾUṣūl ʾIʿtiqād Ahl Al-Sunnah 1:163
author: Imām Mālik

title: The Man Who Was Abducted by Jinn
quote: A man from his people, from the Anṣār, went out one night to pray ʿIshāʾ with his people [during the Caliphate of ʿUmar], but some jinn abducted him, and he went missing. His wife went to ʿUmar and told him [of her missing husband]. ʿUmar asked his people about him, and they confirmed that he had gone out to pray ʿIshāʾ but had gone missing. [ʿUmar] told the woman to wait for four years. When four years had passed, the woman went back to ʿUmar and informed him [of her case]. He asked her people and they confirmed the case. He told her she could now marry, and she did. But her [first] husband returned and raised a dispute [over the marriage] to ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb – Allāh be pleased with him. ʿUmar said, “One of you disappears for ages, his family does not know whether he is alive or not [and then he turns up, making claims]!” The man replied, “O Leader of the Believers, I have an excuse.” He asked, “And what is your excuse?” The man replied, “I went out to pray ʿIshāʾ one night, but some jinn captured me, and I [was held captive] by them for a long time. Then, some Muʾmin – or Muslim (one of the reporters, Saʿīd, was not sure of the exact word used) jinn waged an attack on them, fought them and beat them, and took captives. They took me amongst the captives, but said, ‘We see that you are a Muslim man, and it is not allowed for us to keep you captive.’ So they gave me the choice of staying with them or returning to my family. I chose to return to my family. They set off with me. By night, no one would say anything to me; but by day there would be a stick I would follow.” ʿUmar – Allāh be pleased with him – asked the man, “What was your food when you were amongst them?” He replied, “Difficult [stolen?] food, and what had not had the name of Allāh mentioned over it.” ʿUmar asked, “And what did you drink when you were amongst them?” He replied, “Al-jadaf (a type of drink that does not ferment).” ʿUmar ruled that the man had a choice to either get back the ṣadāq (dowry) he had given [and have his marriage stay nullified], or take back his wife.
intro: ʿAbd Al-Raḥmān b. Abī Laylā reports:
source: Al-Bayhaqi, Al-Sunan Al-Kubrā 7:445,446. Al-Albānī graded its chain of transmission ṣaḥīḥ in Irwā Al-Ghalīl 4:151
author: ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb

title: Runaway Scholars
quote: It has reached me that the scholars of old would practice what they learned when they learned it, and through practicing they would become occupied, and because of being occupied, they would be missed, and when they were missed they were sought after, and when they were sought after they would flee.
intro: It is reported that Al-Fuḍayl b. ʿAyyāḍ – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Al-Fuḍayl b. ʿAyyāḍ

title: Will You Not Fight? [Fitnah]
quote: Will you not fight? For you are one of the people of shūrā (to be consulted in these affairs), and you are more deserving of this matter (leadership) than others? So he replied: I will not fight until you bring me a sword with two eyes and lips that recognizes the believer from the unbeliever, for I have done jihād and I know jihād.
intro: It is reported that it was said to Saʿd b. Abī Waqqāṣ – Allāh be pleased with him – during the time of fitnah:
author: Saʿd b. Abī Waqqās

title: The Comfortable Home
quote: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said: There is no comfort or rest for the believer until he meets Allāh.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd – Allāh be pleased with him – said: There is no comfort or rest for the believer until he meets Allāh.
author: Masrūq

title: Speech Therapy [Exhortation]
quote: Woe to you, say what is good and you will reap gain, and be silent from speaking evil and you will be safe.
intro: It is reported that a man saw Ibn ʿAbbās – Allāh be pleased with them – holding the tip of his tongue saying:

title: Comprehensive Reliance
quote: Tawakkul (reliance and trust in Allah) comprehends all of Īmān.
intro: Saʿīd b. Jubayr – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Saʿīd b. Jubayr

title: Seeking Reputation
quote: Whoever tries to make people hear of his deeds, Allāh will make [some] of His creation hear of him (expose him), and belittle him. Ibn ʿUmar then began to cry.
intro: It is reported that ʿAbdullāh b. ʿUmar – Allāh be pleased with them – said:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿUmar

title: Dua from the Bottom of the Heart [Ikhlas]
quote: ʿAbd Al-Raḥmān b. Yazīd reports: Al-Rabīʿ b. Khuthaym used to come to ʿAlqamah on the day of Jumuʿah and they would talk. They would call for me and I would come and talk with them. One day, they sent for me and I came. ʿAlqamah said to me, Have you not heard what Al-Rabīʿ b. Khuthaym has come to us with? I replied, And what is that? He said, [Rabīʿ] said a man from the People of Scripture once said to us, Do you not see how much people supplicate but how seldom their prayers are answered? That is because Allāh does not accept except what is sincere and purely for Him. I said, Well ʿAbdullāh [b. Masʿūd] already said the same. He asked, What did he say? I replied, Haven’t you heard him say, By Him other than whom there is none worthy of worship, Allāh does not accept [the deed] of one who seeks to be heard of, or seen, and nor someone who is playing around, [He only accepts the supplication] of one who calls upon Him from the bottom of his heart. He replied, Indeed, [I have heard him say that].
intro: ʿAbd Al-Raḥmān b. Yazīd reports:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. Masʿūd

title: When did you Get so Harsh?
quote: I saw Zuhayr b. Muʿāwiyah come to Zāʾidah b. Qudāmah (d.160H) and speak to him to get him to narrate ḥadīth to a man, so Zāʾidah asked, Is he from Ahl Al-Sunnah? Zuhayr replied, I do not know that he has any bidʿah. Zāʾidah said, No, that is another matter! Is he from Ahl Al-Sunnah ? Zuhayr said, Since when have people become like this? Zāʾidah replied, Since when did people curse Abū Bakr and ʿUmar?!
intro: Aḥmad b. Yūnus reports:
source: Al-Khaṭīb Al-Baghdādī, Al-Jāmiʿ li Akhlāq Al-Rāwī #754.
author: Zāʾidah b. Qudāmah

title: Pimping the Religion
quote: How will you be (in the end times) when you open up your religion like the woman who opens her legs to expose her qubul not stopping anyone from coming to her?
intro: It is reported that Ḥudhayfah – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
source: Abū ʿAmr Al-Dānī, Al-Sunan Al-Wāridah fī Al-Fitan #241
author: Ḥudhayfah b. Al-Yamān

title: Believers and Chameleons
quote: Verily, you will find the believer (muʾmin) consistent time after time, upon one way, showing the same face; [whereas] you will find the hypocrite (munāfiq) changing colors, trying to be like everyone around him, running with every wind.
intro: It is reported that Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir Al-ʿIlm #1936
author: Al-Ḥasan Al-Baṣrī

title: Sometimes the Manners Surpassed Knowledge
quote: We used to sometimes come to a scholar and what we learned of his manners was more beloved to us than the knowledge we took from him.
intro: It is reported that Imām Al-Zuhrī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Imām Al-Zuhrī

title: Try to Meet Allah with This
quote: Please write down all knowledge for me. Ibn ʿUmar replied: Knowledge is vast, but if you can meet Allāh having kept your back light of the burden of people’s blood, your stomach void of people’s wealth and having kept your tongue from [disparaging] their honour, then do so.
intro: It is reported that a man once wrote to Ibn ʿUmar – Allāh be pleased with them:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿUmar

title: Mixing with People of Good Character
quote: Do not mix except with those who have good character; for the one who has good character brings nothing but good, whilst the one who has bad character brings nothing but evil.
intro: It is reported that Al-Fuḍayl b. ʿAyyāḍ – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Al-Fuḍayl b. ʿAyyāḍ

title: Stars on Earth
quote: The example of the scholars on earth is that of the stars in the sky: when they appear, the people are guided (they navigate using the stars), but when they disappear, the people get confused and lost.
intro: It is reported that Abū Muslim Al-Khawlānī – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Abū Muslim Al-Khawlānī

title: The Humble Elite
quote: It is reported that Imām Al-Shāfʿee – Allāh have mercy on him – said, The loftiest in status are those who do not know their own status, and the most virtuous of them are those who do not know their own virtue.
intro: It is reported that Imām Al-Shāfʿee – Allāh have mercy on him – said, The loftiest in status are those who do not know their own status, and the most virtuous of them are those who do not know their own virtue.
author: Imām Al-Shāfiʿī

title: Abu Bakr and Umar with The Prophet Today
quote: It is reported from Sufyān b. ʿUyainah – Allāh have mercy on him – that a man once asked ʿAlī b. Ḥusayn b. ʿAlī b. ʾAbī Ṭālib – Allāh have mercy on him, What was the status of Abū Bakr and ʿUmar with Rasūlullāh – Allāh’s praise and peace be upon him? He replied, Their place with him is like their place with him today (i.e. where they are in their graves).
intro: It is reported from Sufyān b. ʿUyainah – Allāh have mercy on him – that a man once asked ʿAlī b. Ḥusayn b. ʿAlī b. ʾAbī Ṭālib – Allāh have mercy on him, What was the status of Abū Bakr and ʿUmar with Rasūlullāh – Allāh’s praise and peace be upon him? He replied, Their place with him is like their place with him today (i.e. where they are in their graves).
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawāhir Al-ʿIlm 4:254
author: ʿAlī b. Ḥusayn Zayn Al-ʿĀbidīn

title: The Scholar-Messenger and The Roman King
quote: It is reported from Al-Shaʿbī – Allāh have mercy on him – that he said: ʿAbdul-Malik [ibn Marwān, the Khalīfah] once sent me to the king of the Romans, and I stayed with him for a number of days. When I wanted to leave, he asked me, Are you from the House of [your] king (his family)? I replied, I am just a man from the Arabs. He gave me a parchment and said, Deliver this to your companion (the Caliph). When ʿAbdul-Malik had read it, he said to me, Do you know what it says? I replied, No. He said, It says in it, I wonder at a people who have made other than this man king over them. I said, By Allāh, if I had known I would not have carried it to you. [The Roman king] only said this because he has not seen you. ʿAbdul-Malik said, Rather, he envied me for having you, and was trying to incite me to kill you. This reached the Roman king; and when it did, he said, I wanted nothing but this.
intro: It is reported from Al-Shaʿbī – Allāh have mercy on him – that he said:
author: Al-Shaʿbī

title: Hiding Righteous Tears
quote: It is reported from Ḥammād b. Zayd that he said, I never saw a man who used to smile more at people than ʾAyyūb.
intro: It is reported from Ḥammād b. Zayd that he said, I never saw a man who used to smile more at people than ʾAyyūb.
author: Ayyūb Al-Sakhtiyānī

title: Never Give Up Re­pent­ing
quote: It is reported that a man came to ʿAlī – Allāh be pleased with him – and asked, What do you think about a man who committed a sin? He replied, He must seek Allāh’s forgiveness and repent to Him. [The man] said, He did that, but then sinned again? [ʿAlī] said, He must seek Allāh’s forgiveness and repent to Him. The man again said, He did that, but returned to sin . [ʿAlī] said, He must seek Allāh’s forgiveness and repent to Him. The man said for the fourth time, He did, but then sinned again. ʿAlī then said, Until when? Then he said, He must seek Allāh’s forgiveness and repent to Him; and not give up until it is Shayṭān who is defeated [overcome].
intro: It is reported that a man came to ʿAlī – Allāh be pleased with him – and asked, What do you think about a man who committed a sin? He replied, He must seek Allāh’s forgiveness and repent to Him. [The man] said, He did that, but then sinned again? [ʿAlī] said, He must seek Allāh’s forgiveness and repent to Him. The man again said, He did that, but returned to sin . [ʿAlī] said, He must seek Allāh’s forgiveness and repent to Him. The man said for the fourth time, He did, but then sinned again. ʿAlī then said, Until when? Then he said, He must seek Allāh’s forgiveness and repent to Him; and not give up until it is Shayṭān who is defeated [overcome].
source: Hunād b. Al-Sarī, Kitāb Al-Zuhd article 910
author: ʿAlī b. Abī Ṭālib

title: Filthy Silk
quote: It is reported that a shaykh (older man) once came and greeted ‘Alī – Allāh be pleased with him, wearing a cloak decorated with silk at the front. He said to the man, What is this filth under your beard? The man looked around and said, I do not see anything. Another man said, He means the silk embroidery. The shaykh said, In that case, we will throw it away and never wear [such a thing] again.
intro: It is reported that a shaykh (older man) once came and greeted ‘Alī – Allāh be pleased with him, wearing a cloak decorated with silk at the front. He said to the man, What is this filth under your beard? The man looked around and said, I do not see anything. Another man said, He means the silk embroidery. The shaykh said, In that case, we will throw it away and never wear [such a thing] again.
author: ʿAlī b. Abī Ṭālib

title: Refusing Good Treatment
quote: A cushion was once passed to ‘Alī – Allāh be pleased with him, upon which he sat, and said, None but a donkey refuses good treatment (honour/respect).
intro: A cushion was once passed to ‘Alī – Allāh be pleased with him, upon which he sat, and said, None but a donkey refuses good treatment (honour/respect).
author: ʿAlī b. Abī Ṭālib

title: Required Learning [What is a Muslim Required to Study?]
quote: It is not [the expert knowledge of hadith and fiqh] you are seeking. Seeking knowledge is an obligation when a man comes to something concerning his religion (dīn): he is to ask about it until he knows it.
intro: It is reported that H asan b. Rabī’ asked ‘Abdullāh b. Al-Mubārak – Allāh have mercy on him – about the meaning of the hadith, “Seeking knowledge is obligatory upon every Muslim”. He replied:
source: Al-Kha t īb Al-Baghdādī, Al-Faqīh wa Al-Mutafaqqih article 162.
author: Imām Aḥmad

title: Delving into Falsehood (I’ll take what’s good)
quote: Delving into falsehood removes the ability to recognize the truth from the heart.
intro: It is reported that Ibrāhīm b. Ad-ham – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Ibrāhīm b. Adham

title: Mixing or Withdrawing for Gain
quote: The believer mixes with others in order to learn, keeps quiet to keep safe (from sinning), speaks in order to understand, and secludes himself for the attainment (of good).
intro: It is reported that Wahb b. Munabbih – Allāh have mercy on him – used to say:

title: Sincere Faith
quote: Three things are from Imān: when a man has nocturnal emission during a cold night, so he gets up – only Allāh sees him – and he has a full wash (ghusl); when a person fasts on a hot day; and when a man prays in a barren land, where none but Allāh sees him.
intro: It is reported that Abū Hurayrah – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: Abū Hurayrah

title: Your Saved Chat History
quote: Those before you used to consider idle talk to be anything other than the Book of Allāh, or the enjoining of good, or the forbidding of evil, or speaking for the sake of your basic living needs. Do you deny that there are recording angels appointed over you? Sitting on your right and your left? Never is a word said except there is an observer prepared to record? Are you not afraid (ashamed) that your record of words and deeds be spread open only to discover that there is nothing of the hereafter in it?
intro: It is reported that Ya’lā b. ‘Ubayd said, “We entered upon Ibn Sūqah, who said: ‘O nephew, let me relate to you something that will hopefully benefit you; for it benefited me. ‘A t ā b. Abī Rabā h once said to us:'”
author: Aṭā b. Abī Rabāḥ

title: Two Rare Things in Decline
quote: I do not know anything rarer than a good dirham (money earnt lawfully) whose owner spends it on something right, or a brother in Islām in whose company one finds peace. And they are only getting rarer.
intro: It is reported that Yūnus b. ‘Ubayd – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
author: Yūnus b. ʿUbayd

title: Five Essential Qualities of a Mufti
quote: No man should appoint himself to issue verdicts (fatwa) until and unless he fulfills five characteristics: First, he should have a [firm and good] intention; for if he does not, he will have no light upon him, and nor will his words. Second, he should be forbearing, tranquil and serene. Third, he should be strong upon what he is involved in, strong in its knowledge. Fourth, he should have sufficient means (wealth), otherwise the people will chew him up. Fifth, he should know people and their ways.
intro: Ibn Ba tt ah records that Imām A h mad – Allāh have mercy on him – said:
source: Ibn Ba tt ah, Ibtāl Al- H iyal p24.
author: Imām Aḥmad

title: A Preserved State of Knowledge
quote: You will remain upon good as long as you do not start approving of what you used to repudiate and as long as you do not repudiate what you used to know (to be right), and as long as your scholar (the knowledgeable) can speak amongst you without fear.
intro: It is reported that ʿAdī b. Ḥātim – Allāh be pleased with him – said:
author: ʿAdī b. Ḥātim

title: Sayyiduna Umar on Abu Bakr and Bilal
quote: Abū Bakr is our sayyid (master/leader), and he freed our sayyid – meaning Bilāl.
intro: Jābir b. ‘Abdillāh reports that ‘Umar b. Al-Kha tt āb – Allāh be pleased with them – used to say:
source: Al-Bukhārī, Al-Sa h ī h , hadith 3754.
author: ʿUmar b. Al-Khaṭṭāb

title: Seven Hundred Deadly Sins
quote: “How many kabā`ir (major, deadly sins) are there? Seven?” He replied, “They are closer to seven hundred than seven; except a major sin does not remain so if one (truly) seeks forgiveness [from Allāh], and a lesser sin does not remain so if one insists on continuously doing it (i.e. it becomes a major sin).”
intro: Sa’īd b. Jubayr – Allāh have mercy on him – reports that a man once asked Ibn ‘Abbās – Allāh be pleased with them:
author: ʿAbdullāh b. ʿAbbās

title: Respect for Scholars and Ahl Al-Bayt
quote: Zayd b. Thābit – Allāh be pleased with him – once mounted [his horse or camel] to ride it, so Ibn ‘Abbās – Allāh be pleased with them – took hold of the reins [to serve him by leading his ride]. When he did this, Zayd said (out of respect for him), “Don’t do this o cousin of Allāh’s Messenger – Allāh’s praise and peace be upon him.” Ibn ‘Abbās said, “This is what we have been told to do with our scholars (i.e. respect them and serve them).” Zayd said, “Show me your hand.” Ibn ‘Abbās held out his hand, and Zayd kissed it and said, “And this is what we have been told to do with the Ahl Al-Bayt (Family) of our Prophet – Allāh’s praise and peace be upon him.”
intro: It is reported from Al-Sha’bī that he said:
source: Abū Bakr Al-Daynūrī, Al-Mujālasah wa Jawaāhir Al-‘Ilm 4:146, et al
author: Zayd b. Thābit

title: When Blessings Turn to Blight
